one_time_description: Settlement of Treaty Annuities Specific Claim (TAC) from Crown-Indigenous Relations; also water treatment plant transferred from Tribal Chiefs Ventures Inc. valued at $9,547,962 (non-cash)
economic_notes: Investments in 681415 Alberta Ltd. (Kehewin Gasification), Kwayaskwaw Contracting Ltd., Seven Lakes Oilfield Services LP (8.33%), Pimee Well Servicing LP (16.66%), Pimee Well Services Ltd. (16.66% cost method). Oil and gas royalties administered by Government of Canada. Legacy Trust established to hold TAC settlement funds and generate sustainable income.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (ended March 31, 2020). Comparative 2019 figures restated due to correction of an error (Note 20) reducing opening accumulated surplus by $1,297,200. Major one-time items: $3,335,526 Treaty Annuities Specific Claim settlement (held in trust at year-end, transferred to Legacy Trust in June 2020) and non-cash transfer of water treatment plant from Tribal Chiefs Ventures Inc. worth $9,547,962 (inflating both revenue and TCA significantly). Three trusts excluded from consolidation: Kehewin Legacy Trust, Kehewin Treaty Land Entitlement Trust, Kehewin Specific Claims Settlement Trust. Two wholly-owned subsidiaries (681415 Alberta Ltd., Kwayaskwaw Contracting Ltd.) and two partnerships accounted for under modified equity method. Lawsuit liability of $1,925,007 recorded in AP; elders lodge fully impaired ($694,340). No schedule of remuneration provided in extracted text. Restricted cash includes $3,335,526 funds held in trust (TAC settlement), $7,119,592 capital projects, $454,073 CMHC. Current liabilities calculated as bank indebtedness ($2,327,872) + accounts payable ($8,769,442). Total government transfers per deferred revenue table: $53,827,429.
